name: credit_union_algo_debank_political_speech
premise: Member-owned Riverstone Credit Union CEO Pat Kim discovers that a new fraud model flags accounts linked to lawful but controversial political donations as higher risk for mule activity. Compliance officer Dana Ruiz shows that flagged members skew toward a single protest movement, though absolute dollars are tiny. Freezing review could protect elderly members from scams; leaving the model live may quietly debank activists before an election. NCUA examiners arrive Monday; vendor contracts penalize disabling fraud layers without cause.

agents.name: Pat Kim
agents.identity: I am the CEO accountable to member-owners for safety, inclusion, and reputational trust in a politically divided region.
agents.goal: I want fraud defenses that do not launder viewpoint discrimination through opaque risk scores.

agents.name: Dana Ruiz
agents.identity: I am the compliance officer translating BSA expectations, model documentation, and fair-lending norms into operational controls.
agents.goal: I want any rule that touches political behavior reviewed by humans before accounts are restricted, even if queues back up.

choices: I will disable the donation-linked risk feature until fair lending counsel signs off, accepting higher fraud exposure over the weekend.
I will keep the model active but route all political-donation flags to a senior human panel with published SLAs, accepting that weekend staffing may miss some reviews.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Member advocates are threatening a press conference at noon. Pat Kim must choose between disabling the feature or keeping it with human review. What does Pat Kim do?
